title: esenbeckia grandiflora subsp. grandiflora
intro: Esenbeckia grandiflora subsp. grandiflora, also known as the 'Yellow Bird Tree', is a tropical plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant's extracts and essential oils have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as to treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. Esenbeckia grandiflora subsp. grandiflora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine.
